title: Architectural Layers of a Cloud Compute and Storage Platform
description: A layered diagram showing the core components and their relationships in a typical cloud computing architecture, focusing on the separation between compute and storage resources and how they serve user applications.
elements: Storage Service Layer (Object, Block, File Storage)
Physical Infrastructure Layer (Servers, Hypervisors, Storage Hardware, Network)
relationships: User requests flow through the Management API to provision resources
Compute services run on and are abstracted from the Physical Infrastructure
Compute services read from and write to Storage services
Storage services are provided by and abstracted from the Physical Infrastructure
